ddls:
CREATE TABLE "items" (
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each item */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the item */,
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* ID of the owner of the item (can be another item or a top-level category) */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category to which the item belongs */,
  "subcategory_id" INTEGER /* ID of the subcategory to which the item belongs */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the item */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Current quantity of the item in stock */,
  "reorder_level" INTEGER /* Threshold for when to reorder the item */,
  "reorder_quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity to reorder when the stock falls below the reorder level */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier of the item */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last time the item's details were updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the item (e.g., active, out of stock, discontinued) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_items_owner_id FOREIGN KEY ("owner_id") REFERENCES owners ("owner_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_items_subcategory_id FOREIGN KEY ("subcategory_id") REFERENCES subcategories ("subcategory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_items_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "owners" (
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each owner */,
  "owner_name" TEXT /* Name of the owner */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the owner */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the owner */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the owner is currently active or not */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("owner_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent category (for hierarchical structure) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the category is currently active or not */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_categories_parent_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subcategories" (
  "subcategory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subcategory */,
  "subcategory_name" TEXT /* Name of the subcategory */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category to which the subcategory belongs */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the subcategory */,
  "parent_subcategory_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent subcategory (for hierarchical structure) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the subcategory is currently active or not */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subcategory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subcategories_parent_subcategory_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_subcategory_id") REFERENCES subcategories ("subcategory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subcategories_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "item_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history event */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the item that was changed */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change occurred */,
  "changed_by" TEXT /* User who made the change */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  "previous_value" TEXT /* Previous value before the change */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value after the change */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the history entry is currently active or not */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_item_history_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("item_id") REFERENCES items ("item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_item_history_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, manager, user) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is currently active or not */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* User permissions in JSON format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
